heading: Expenses for household and dependent care services necessary for gainful employment
label: 21 Expenses for household and dependent care services necessary for gainful employment
notes: Editorial Notes
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 21 was renumbered .section 15 of this title

Amendments2025—Subsec. (a)(2). amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of paragraph (1), the term ‘applicable percentage’ means 35 percent reduced (but not below 20 percent) by 1 percentage point for each $2,000 (or fraction thereof) by which the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income for the taxable year exceeds $15,000.”Pub. L. 119–212021—Subsec. (g). , added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 117–2, § 9631(a)Subsec. (h). , added subsec. (h).Pub. L. 117–2, § 9631(b)2007—Subsec. (e)(5). substituted “section 152(e)(4)(A)” for “section 152(e)(3)(A)” in concluding provisions.Pub. L. 110–1722005—Subsec. (b)(1)(B). inserted “(as defined in section 152, determined without regard to subsections (b)(1), (b)(2), and (d)(1)(B))” after “dependent of the taxpayer”.Pub. L. 109–1352004—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “In the case of an individual for which there are 1 or more qualifying individuals (as defined in subsection (b)(1)) with respect to such individual” for “In the case of an individual who maintains a household which includes as a member one or more qualifying individuals (as defined in subsection (b)(1))”.Pub. L. 108–311, § 203(a)Subsec. (b)(1). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The term ‘qualifying individual’ means—Pub. L. 108–311, § 203(b)“(A) a dependent of the taxpayer who is under the age of 13 and with respect to whom the taxpayer is entitled to a deduction under section 151(c),“(B) a dependent of the taxpayer who is physically or mentally incapable of caring for himself, or“(C) the spouse of the taxpayer, if he is physically or mentally incapable of caring for himself.”Subsec. (e)(1). , amended heading and text of par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “An individual shall be treated as maintaining a household for any period only if over half the cost of maintaining the household for such period is furnished by such individual (or, if such individual is married during such period, is furnished by such individual and his spouse).”Pub. (f)(6). substituted provision disallowing a credit for any amount paid by a taxpayer to an individual with respect to whom, for the taxable year, a deduction under section 151(e) is allowable either to the taxpayer or his spouse or who is a child of the taxpayer who has not attained the age of 19 at the close of the taxpayer year and defining “taxpayer year” for provision disallowing a credit for any amount paid by the taxpayer to an individual bearing a relationship described in section 152(a)(1) through (8), or a dependent described in section 152(a)(9), except that a credit was allowed for an amount paid by a taxpayer to an individual with respect to whom, for the taxable year of the taxpayer in which the service was performed, neither the taxpayer nor his spouse was entitled to a deduction under section 151(e), provided the service constituted employment within the meaning of section 3121(b).Pub. L. 95–600

Program To Increase Public Awareness, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–508, title XI, § 11114104 Stat. 1388–414Nov. 5, 1990“Not later than the first calendar year following the date of the enactment of this subtitle [], the Secretary of the Treasury, or the Secretary’s delegate, shall establish a taxpayer awareness program to inform the taxpaying public of the availability of the credit for dependent care allowed under section 21 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and the earned income credit and child health insurance under section 32 of such Code. Such public awareness program shall be designed to assure that individuals who may be eligible are informed of the availability of such credit and filing procedures. The Secretary shall use appropriate means of communication to carry out the provisions of this section.”Nov. 5, 1990

Program To Increase Public Awareness, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–508, title XI, § 11114104 Stat. 1388–414Nov. 5, 1990“Not later than the first calendar year following the date of the enactment of this subtitle [], the Secretary of the Treasury, or the Secretary’s delegate, shall establish a taxpayer awareness program to inform the taxpaying public of the availability of the credit for dependent care allowed under section 21 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and the earned income credit and child health insurance under section 32 of such Code. Such public awareness program shall be designed to assure that individuals who may be eligible are informed of the availability of such credit and filing procedures. The Secretary shall use appropriate means of communication to carry out the provisions of this section.”Nov. 5, 1990

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of an individual for which there are 1 or more qualifying individuals (as defined in subsection (b)(1)) with respect to such individual, there shall be allowed as a credit against the tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year an amount equal to the applicable percentage of the employment-related expenses (as defined in subsection (b)(2)) paid by such individual during the taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: reduced (but not below 35 percent) by 1 percentage point for each $2,000 or fraction thereof by which the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income for the taxable year exceeds $15,000, and

children.label: B
children.text: further reduced (but not below 20 percent) by 1 percentage point for each $2,000 ($4,000 in the case of a joint return) or fraction thereof by which the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income for the taxable year exceeds $75,000 ($150,000 in the case of a joint return).

children.heading: Definitions of qualifying individual and employment-related expenses
children.label: b Definitions of qualifying individual and employment-related expenses
A
children.text: a dependent of the taxpayer (as defined in section 152(a)(1)) who has not attained age 13,

children.label: B
children.text: a dependent of the taxpayer (as defined in section 152, determined without regard to subsections (b)(1), (b)(2), and (d)(1)(B)) who is physically or mentally incapable of caring for himself or herself and who has the same principal place of abode as the taxpayer for more than one-half of such taxable year, or

children.label: C
children.text: the spouse of the taxpayer, if the spouse is physically or mentally incapable of caring for himself or herself and who has the same principal place of abode as the taxpayer for more than one-half of such taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: expenses for household services, and

children.label: ii
children.text: expenses for the care of a qualifying individual.

children.label: i
children.text: a qualifying individual described in paragraph (1)(A), or

children.label: ii
children.text: a qualifying individual (not described in paragraph (1)(A)) who regularly spends at least 8 hours each day in the taxpayer’s household.

children.label: i
children.text: such center complies with all applicable laws and regulations of a State or unit of local government, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirements of subparagraph (B) are met.

children.label: i
children.text: provides care for more than six individuals (other than individuals who reside at the facility), and

children.label: ii
children.text: receives a fee, payment, or grant for providing services for any of the individuals (regardless of whether such facility is operated for profit).

children.heading: Dollar limit on amount creditable
children.label: c Dollar limit on amount creditable

children.text: $3,000 if there is 1 qualifying individual with respect to the taxpayer for such taxable year, or
$6,000 if there are 2 or more qualifying individuals with respect to the taxpayer for such taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an individual who is not married at the close of such year, such individual’s earned income for such year, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an individual who is married at the close of such year, the lesser of such individual’s earned income or the earned income of his spouse for such year.

children.heading: Special rule for spouse who is a student or incapable of caring for himself
children.label: 2 Special rule for spouse who is a student or incapable of caring for himself
A
children.text: $250 if subsection (c)(1) applies for the taxable year, or

children.label: B
children.text: $500 if subsection (c)(2) applies for the taxable year.

children.heading: Place of abode
children.label: 1 Place of abode
children.text: An individual shall not be treated as having the same principal place of abode of the taxpayer if at any time during the taxable year of the taxpayer the relationship between the individual and the taxpayer is in violation of local law.

children.heading: Married couples must file joint return
children.label: 2 Married couples must file joint return
children.text: If the taxpayer is married at the close of the taxable year, the credit shall be allowed under subsection (a) only if the taxpayer and his spouse file a joint return for the taxable year.

children.heading: Marital status
children.label: 3 Marital status
children.text: An individual legally separated from his spouse under a decree of divorce or of separate maintenance shall not be considered as married.

children.label: i
children.text: maintains as his home a household which constitutes for more than one-half of the taxable year the principal place of abode of a qualifying individual, and

children.label: ii
children.text: furnishes over half of the cost of maintaining such household during the taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: during the last 6 months of such taxable year such individual’s spouse is not a member of such household,

children.heading: Special dependency test in case of divorced parents, etc.
children.label: 5 Special dependency test in case of divorced parents, etc.
A
children.text: section 152(e) applies to any child with respect to any calendar year, and

children.label: B
children.text: such child is under the age of 13 or is physically or mentally incapable of caring for himself,

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to whom, for the taxable year, a deduction under section 151(c) (relating to deduction for personal exemptions for dependents) is allowable either to the taxpayer or his spouse, or

children.label: B
children.text: who is a child of the taxpayer (within the meaning of section 152(f)(1)) who has not attained the age of 19 at the close of the taxable year.

children.heading: Student
children.label: 7 Student
children.text: The term “student” means an individual who during each of 5 calendar months during the taxable year is a full-time student at an educational organization.

children.heading: Educational organization
children.label: 8 Educational organization
children.text: The term “educational organization” means an educational organization described in section 170(b)(1)(A)(ii).

children.heading: Identifying information required with respect to service provider
children.label: 9 Identifying information required with respect to service provider
A
children.text: the name, address, and taxpayer identification number of such person are included on the return claiming the credit, or

children.label: B
children.text: if such person is an organization described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from tax under section 501(a), the name and address of such person are included on the return claiming the credit.

children.heading: Identifying information required with respect to qualifying individuals
children.label: 10 Identifying information required with respect to qualifying individuals
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section with respect to any qualifying individual unless the TIN of such individual is included on the return claiming the credit.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Credit made refundable
children.label: 1 Credit made refundable
children.text: If the taxpayer (in the case of a joint return, either spouse) has a principal place of abode in the United States (determined as provided in section 32) for more than one-half of the taxable year, the credit allowed under subsection (a) shall be treated as a credit allowed under subpart C (and not allowed under this subpart).

children.heading: Increase in dollar limit on amount creditable
children.label: 2 Increase in dollar limit on amount creditable
A
children.text: by substituting “$8,000” for “$3,000” in paragraph (1) thereof, and

children.label: B
children.text: by substituting “$16,000” for “$6,000” in paragraph (2) thereof.

children.label: A
children.text: by substituting “50 percent” for “35 percent”, and

children.label: B
children.text: by substituting “$125,000” for “$15,000”.

children.heading: Application of phaseout to high income individuals
children.label: 4 Application of phaseout to high income individuals

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subsection (a)(2) shall be applied by substituting “the phaseout percentage” for “20 percent”.

children.heading: Phaseout percentage
children.label: B Phaseout percentage
children.text: The term “phaseout percentage” means 20 percent reduced (but not below zero) by 1 percentage point for each $2,000 (or fraction thereof) by which the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income for the taxable year exceeds $400,000.

children.heading: Application of credit in possessions
children.label: h Application of credit in possessions

children.heading: Payment to possessions with mirror code tax systems
children.label: 1 Payment to possessions with mirror code tax systems
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to each possession of the United States with a mirror code tax system amounts equal to the loss (if any) to that possession by reason of the application of this section (determined without regard to this subsection) with respect to taxable years beginning in or with 2021. Such amounts shall be determined by the Secretary based on information provided by the government of the respective possession.

children.heading: Payments to other possessions
children.label: 2 Payments to other possessions
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to each possession of the United States which does not have a mirror code tax system amounts estimated by the Secretary as being equal to the aggregate benefits that would have been provided to residents of such possession by reason of this section with respect to taxable years beginning in or with 2021 if a mirror code tax system had been in effect in such possession. The preceding sentence shall not apply unless the respective possession has a plan, which has been approved by the Secretary, under which such possession will promptly distribute such payments to its residents.

children.heading: Coordination with credit allowed against United States income taxes
children.label: 3 Coordination with credit allowed against United States income taxes
A
children.text: to whom a credit is allowable against taxes imposed by a possession with a mirror code tax system by reason of this section, or

children.label: B
children.text: who is eligible for a payment under a plan described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Mirror code tax system
children.label: 4 Mirror code tax system
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “mirror code tax system” means, with respect to any possession of the United States, the income tax system of such possession if the income tax liability of the residents of such possession under such system is determined by reference to the income tax laws of the United States as if such possession were the United States.

children.heading: Treatment of payments
children.label: 5 Treatment of payments
children.text: For purposes of , United States Code, the payments under this subsection shall be treated in the same manner as a refund due from a credit provision referred to in subsection (b)(2) of such section.section 1324 of title 31
